title: cerinthe major var. oranensis
intro: Cerinthe major var. oranensis, also known as Honey Flower or Blue Shrimp, is a flowering plant in the borage family that is native to North Africa and the Mediterranean region. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, as well as its ability to stimulate digestion and improve gut health. The health benefits of Cerinthe major var. oranensis include reducing inflammation and improving symptoms of gastrointestinal disorders, such as irritable bowel syndrome. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, tinctures, and infusions to support digestive health and alleviate symptoms of digestive issues.
